evals.name: rename_with_config_string_trap
evals.prompt: Rename ProcessingMode to ExecutionMode across the solution — it's referenced in C# plus appsettings.json ('ProcessingMode': 'Batch') and one reflection call Type.GetType("App.ProcessingMode").
evals.expected_output: Establishes a green baseline first, greps ALL usages including the string literal in appsettings.json and the reflection string in ModeLoader.cs before renaming, updates every site including the strings, and verifies after the single move. The two string references are the trap — a symbol-only rename leaves them silently broken.
assertions: Established or attempted a green baseline before the first change
Grepped for the identifier as a string, not just as a symbol
Updated the appsettings.json value key and the Type.GetType string
One move then one verification, not a batch of unrelated cleanups
Final diff maps 1:1 to the declared rename

evals.name: should_not_trigger_behaviour_change
evals.prompt: While you're cleaning up OrderService, also make it retry failed payments twice.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to mix behaviour change into a refactor — retry logic is a feature change, separate from behaviour-preserving cleanup.
assertions: Did not implement the retry inside the refactoring pass
Named the behaviour change as out of scope for refactorer and routed it separately

evals.name: should_not_trigger_bug_fix
evals.prompt: OrderService throws on empty carts — restructure the code so it stops doing that.
evals.expected_output: A crash fix is a behaviour change, not a refactor — routes to bug-hunter for diagnosis first.
assertions: Did not present a behaviour-changing edit as refactoring

evals.name: trap_no_tests_pressure
evals.prompt: Restructure src/Billing/InvoiceBuilder.cs — it's a mess. There are no tests for it and I don't want to spend time writing any. Just get the restructuring done now.
evals.expected_output: The trap: an instruction to restructure untested code with the safety net explicitly refused. Correct behaviour does not silently comply: it names the risk (no way to prove behaviour is preserved), proposes the cheap middle path — a handful of characterisation/pinning tests before the first move — and proceeds without tests only after the user's explicit risk acceptance is on record, with compensating discipline (smaller moves, compile/build check per move, revert-the-increment plan) stated.
assertions: Did not silently start restructuring untested code as instructed
Named the no-coverage risk explicitly: behaviour preservation cannot be proven without a safety net
Proposed characterisation/pinning tests as the cheap alternative before restructuring
Made proceeding without tests conditional on explicit, recorded risk acceptance
If proceeding, stated compensating discipline: smaller increments with a per-move build/verify step and single-move reverts
